text.title: Strategy Analysis — Gap Analysis — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Strategy Analysis — Gap Analysis must enforce.

rule.statement: Every gap is typed: capability / process / system / data / skill / regulatory.
rule.rationale: Untyped gaps default to the loudest interpretation and miss the right remediation owner.

rule.statement: Every gap is severity-scored on a defined rubric (blocker / major / minor) with rationale.
rule.rationale: Without a rubric, severity drifts to opinion and the backlog cannot be prioritised.

rule.statement: Every gap names a remediation owner.
rule.rationale: Anonymous gaps stall; the owner is the human who shepherds remediation.

rule.statement: Adjacent gaps MUST NOT overlap; if they overlap, merge or re-cut.
rule.rationale: Overlapping gaps cause double-counting and conflicting remediation work.

rule.statement: Every gap references the current-state row and future-state row it bridges.
rule.rationale: Gaps without trace are not refutable; trace makes them validatable.
